title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Technical documentation tidybrreg R package (v0.2.0). Covers complete data flow Norwegian Business Registry API parsing, storage, panel construction, time series output.

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Brønnøysund Register Centre maintains Norway’s Central Coordinating Register Legal Entities (Enhetsregisteret) data.brreg.. API exposes three registries: registry multiple access patterns: CSV JSON bulk endpoints enheter/underenheter return different column sets. JSON carries fields absent CSV (e.g. kapital.*, vedtektsfestetFormaal, aktivitet, paategninger). CSV may carry fields absent JSON (e.g. additional registration dates, dissolution dates). Neither superset . roller registry CSV bulk download. /roller/totalbestand endpoint returns gzipped JSON array element mirrors per-entity /enheter/{orgnr}/roller response: deeply nested rollegrupper → roller → person/enhet structure.

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: data entering package passes one two pipelines. produce flat tibbles atomic columns (list columns). apply rename--coerce step end.

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Used brreg_entity() brreg_search(). brreg API returns nested JSON entity. flatten_json() recursively walks JSON tree produces flat named list dot-notation keys (forretningsadresse.kommune). rename_from_dict() maps known keys via field_dict tibble (49 rows: api_path → col_name + type). Unknown keys pass auto-generated snake_case names. coerce_types() casts dict-mapped column declared type. 49 dict-mapped columns appear every output row, even absent API response (filled typed NA). guarantees stable column contract across entities.
code: API JSON → flatten_json()  → rename_from_dict() → coerce_types() → tibble            recursive dot     field_dict lookup     Date/integer/            notation          + to_snake() for       logical cast                              unknown fields

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Used brreg_download() brreg_snapshot(). Three sub-pipelines, ending rename_and_coerce(): CSV path (parse_bulk_csv): JSON path (parse_bulk_json): Roller path (parse_roles_bulk):
code: .csv.gz → readr::read_csv(col_types = all character) → rename_and_coerce() .json.gz → jsonlite::fromJSON(flatten = TRUE)          → flatten_list_columns()  → drop_hal_links() → rename_and_coerce() .json.gz → jsonlite::fromJSON(simplifyVector = FALSE)          → for each entity: flatten_roles(entity, org_nr)          → bind_rows()

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: jsonlite::fromJSON(flatten = TRUE) expands nested JSON objects dot-notation columns leaves JSON arrays R list columns. brreg JSON bulk download produces six types list columns: flatten_cell() dispatches runtime type: atomic vectors collapsed paste(), data frames serialized JSON strings (preserving fields later extraction), empty lists NULLs become NA_character_. produces flat tibble zero list columns. HAL _links columns metadata API navigation, entity data. dropped drop_hal_links() (regex column names ending links).

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: CSV JSON paths share function. two things: Rename: Look column name (lowercased) field_dict$api_path. found, rename field_dict$col_name. found, apply to_snake() (camelCase/dot.notation → snake_case). Zero columns dropped. Coerce: dict-mapped column, cast declared type: Date via .Date(), integer via .integer(), logical via .logical(), character via .character().

what: Why CSV and JSON produce different columns
title: tidybrreg architecture
text: brreg API guarantee column parity CSV JSON bulk downloads. practice: 65 columns shared two formats. ~25 columns appear CSV (additional registration dates, dissolution dates, foreign insolvency fields). ~2-5 columns appear JSON (kapital., hjelpeenhetskode., respons_klasse). sets shift time brreg adds fields one format . package handles applying rename--coerce logic whatever columns present, passing unknowns rather hardcoding parity.

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Partitioning Hive-style (snapshot_date=YYYY-MM-DD directory names). arrow::open_dataset() hive_partition(snapshot_date = date32()) reads entire collection lazy dataset partition pruning — filtering snapshot_date skips unneeded files entirely.
code: tools::R_user_dir("tidybrreg", "data")/ ├── enheter/ │   ├── snapshot_date=2024-01-01/ │   │   ├── data.parquet          ← processed flat tibble │   │   └── raw/ │   │       └── enheter_bulk.csv.gz  ← original download (provenance) │   ├── snapshot_date=2024-07-01/ │   │   ├── data.parquet │   │   └── raw/ │   │       └── enheter_bulk.json.gz │   └── ... ├── underenheter/ │   └── snapshot_date=.../ ├── roller/ │   └── snapshot_date=.../ └── manifest.json                 ← provenance catalog

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: package never depends arrow nanoparquet Imports level. Suggests. Internal dispatch: write_parquet_safe() writes tempfile first, renames atomically (crash-safe). read_parquet_safe() reads converts tibble. arrow enables lazy queries (brreg_open() → dplyr::filter() → dplyr::collect()). nanoparquet read/write , lazy queries — functions need lazy access (brreg_open()) require arrow explicitly.
code: parquet_tier() returns:   "arrow"       → arrow::write_parquet(), arrow::read_parquet()   "nanoparquet"  → nanoparquet::write_parquet(), nanoparquet::read_parquet()   "none"         → error with install instructions

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: brreg_import() follows path reads user-supplied file instead downloading. create manifest entry (HTTP metadata record).
code: brreg_snapshot("enheter", format = "json")   │   ├─ brreg_download(type = "enheter", format = "json", type_output = "path")   │    ├─ Downloads .gz to tools::R_user_dir("tidybrreg", "cache")/enheter_bulk.json.gz   │    ├─ Stores ETag in .etag sidecar file   │    └─ Stores httr2 response object in .brregEnv for manifest   │   ├─ Copies raw .gz to snapshot partition dir /raw/   │   ├─ Parses: parse_bulk_json() or parse_bulk_csv() or parse_roles_bulk()   │   ├─ write_parquet_safe() → data.parquet in partition dir   │   └─ write_manifest_entry() → appends to manifest.json

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: manifest.json JSON file downloads array. entry records: last_modified field critical timestamp CDC bridging. represents brreg regenerated bulk file, downloaded . bridge CDC updates without gaps, fetch updates starting last_modified - 1 day (deliberate overlap), deduplicate.

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Requires 2+ snapshots. Produces firm × period panel row entity point time. LOCF (last observation carried forward): target period falls two snapshots, earlier snapshot’s data carries forward. means panel “2024-12-31” shows state “2024-07-01” snapshot later snapshot exists year-end.
code: brreg_panel(frequency = "year", cols = c("employees", "nace_1"))   │   ├─ brreg_snapshots() → list available partitions with dates   │   ├─ generate_year_targets(from, to) → target dates (e.g. 2024-12-31, 2025-12-31)   │   ├─ resolve_snapshot_dates(available, targets) → LOCF mapping   │    Each target date maps to the most recent snapshot on or before it.   │    Target 2024-12-31 with snapshots [2024-01-01, 2024-07-01, 2025-01-01]   │    → uses 2024-07-01 (nearest prior).   │   ├─ Read needed snapshots (arrow lazy or nanoparquet eager)   │   ├─ inner_join with mapping → adds period and snapshot_date columns   │   └─ add_entry_exit() → is_entry (first appearance), is_exit (last appearance)

what: Path B: Single snapshot + CDC replay (brreg_replay)
title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Requires one base snapshot + CDC update stream brreg_updates(include_changes = TRUE). CDC field-level changes available September 2025. date, change type recorded — package can insert/delete entities reconstruct fields changed. pre-2025 field-level changes, use brreg_events() (snapshot diff) instead. Patch field mapping: CDC patches use camelCase slash-separated paths (forretningsadresse/postnummer). lookup_patch_field() converts field_dict column names auto snake_case.
code: brreg_replay(base, updates, target_date = "2025-12-31")   │   ├─ Filter updates to timestamp <= target_date   │   ├─ Sort updates chronologically   │   └─ For each update, by change_type:        "Ny"       → Insert new row (with patch fields if available)        "Endring"  → Update fields in-place via patch        "Sletting" → Delete row        "Fjernet"  → Delete row

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: brreg_series() accepts character vectors variables, functions, grouping: Output columns named {variable}_{function} (e.g. employees_avg, employees_total). .vars = NULL, counts entities per period. return value carries brreg_panel_meta attribute index, key, frequency fields. as_brreg_tsibble() uses convert tsibble automatically: regular = FALSE mandatory brreg snapshots irregularly spaced (daily, weekly, monthly — whatever user accumulated).
code: brreg_series(   .vars = "employees",                              # any column name(s)   .fns  = list(avg = mean, total = sum, sd = sd),   # any summary function(s)   by    = c("legal_form", "municipality_code"),      # any grouping column(s)   frequency = "year" ) brreg_series(.vars = "employees", by = "legal_form") |>   as_brreg_tsibble() # → tsibble with index = period, key = legal_form, regular = FALSE

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Compares two dated snapshots returns tibble events: Change events include value_from value_to — key advantage CDC, provides new values.

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: Follows eurostat package pattern: codes default, labels demand. brreg_label() polymorphic: works data frames (replaces coded columns) character vectors (returns labelled vector). code = argument keeps original codes _code suffixed column. Label lookup sources: - legal_forms (44 rows, bundled) — legal form codes - role_types (18 rows, bundled) — role codes - role_groups (15 rows, bundled) — role group codes - nace_codes (1783 rows, sysdata.rda) — NACE industry codes - sector_codes (33 rows, sysdata.rda) — institutional sector codes - SSB Klass API (live, cached .brregEnv) — NACE sector codes fetched demand sysdata stale
code: brreg_entity("923609016")                    → legal_form = "ASA" brreg_entity("923609016", type = "label")    → legal_form = "Public limited company" brreg_entity("923609016") |> brreg_label()   → legal_form = "Public limited company" brreg_entity("923609016") |>   brreg_label(code = "legal_form")           → legal_form = "Public limited company"                                                legal_form_code = "ASA"  (preserved)

title: tidybrreg architecture
text: enheter/underenheter roller CDC endpoints use different formats: Enheter/Underenheter — HAL JSON: Field-level changes (endringer) flat interleaved array: ["operation", "/path", "value", ...]. parse_patch() restructures tibble columns operation, field, new_value. Roller — CloudEvents: Roller CDC provides entity role changes, actual role data. get new state, fetch /enheter/{orgnr}/roller changed entity.
code: /oppdateringer/enheter?dato=2026-03-15T00:00:00.000Z&size=100&includeChanges=true → { _embedded: { oppdaterteEnheter: [     { oppdateringsid: 123, organisasjonsnummer: "923609016",       endringstype: "Endring", dato: "2026-03-15T10:30:00.000",       endringer: ["replace", "/antallAnsatte", "21500", ...] }   ]}} /oppdateringer/roller?afterTime=2026-03-15T00:00:00.000Z&size=100 → [{ specversion: "1.0", id: "123",      source: "https://data.brreg.no/.../enheter/923609016/roller",      type: "no.brreg.enhetsregisteret.rolle.oppdatert",      time: "2026-03-15T10:30:00.000Z",      data: { organisasjonsnummer: "923609016" } }]

title: Package architecture
Package architecture
text: Flat tidy output. JSON nested objects list columns algorithmically flattened atomic types. Character vectors collapsed "; ", data frames serialized JSON strings, HAL links dropped. CSV JSON paths share rename_and_coerce(). Zero-drop policy. Unknown API fields pass auto snake_case names. arrow::open_dataset(unify_schemas = TRUE) handles schema evolution across snapshot dates. Raw file provenance. Every snapshot stores original .gz alongside processed Parquet. JSON manifest records HTTP headers (Last-Modified data vintage, ETag change detection), file hashes, record counts. Two panel paths. Multi-snapshot diff (brreg_panel()) historical analysis old+new values. CDC replay (brreg_replay()) forward reconstruction single base. See ARCHITECTURE.md complete specification.

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: Every Norwegian legal entity receives unique 9-digit organization number (organisasjonsnummer) upon registration. number uses modulus-11 check digit validation: Numbers starting 8 assigned sub-entities (underenheter), numbers starting 9 main entities (enheter). Historical numbers starting digits exist longer assigned.
code: library(tidybrreg) #> tidybrreg: bulk data not yet downloaded for: underenheter, roller. #> Run brreg_snapshot() for full network/panel support. See ?brreg_status for details. brreg_validate(c("923609016", "984851006", "123456789")) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE FALSE

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: brreg API exposes three distinct registries: Enheter (main entities): legal entity — company, association, government body holds rights, obligations, unit taxation. Underenheter (sub-entities): Physical locations operational units belonging main entity. company offices Oslo Bergen one enhet two underenheter. Sub-entities organization numbers linked parent via overordnetEnhet. Roller (roles): Board members, officers, auditors, accountants, shareholders registered entity. Roles link persons (name + birth date) entities (organization number) entities.

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: organisasjonsform field classifies entities. Common codes: Key distinctions research: (Private limited company): ~300K entities. Minimum NOK 30,000 share capital. Board optional small . ASA (Public limited company): ~220 entities. Listed intending list. Gender quota boards (≥40% gender since 2008). ENK (Sole proprietorship): ~400K entities. board, separate legal personality. Often zero employees. NUF (Norwegian-registered foreign entity): Branch offices foreign companies. Limited data available.
code: legal_forms[legal_forms$code %in% c("AS", "ASA", "ENK", "NUF", "DA", "ANS", "SA", "STI"), ] #> # A tibble: 8 × 4 #>   code  name_no                                 expired name_en                  #>   <chr> <chr>                                   <chr>   <chr>                    #> 1 ANS   Ansvarlig selskap med solidarisk ansvar NA      General partnership (jo… #> 2 AS    Aksjeselskap                            NA      Private limited company  #> 3 ASA   Allmennaksjeselskap                     NA      Public limited company   #> 4 DA    Ansvarlig selskap med delt ansvar       NA      General partnership (sh… #> 5 ENK   Enkeltpersonforetak                     NA      Sole proprietorship      #> 6 NUF   Norskregistrert utenlandsk foretak      NA      Norwegian-registered fo… #> 7 SA    Samvirkeforetak                         NA      Cooperative              #> 8 STI   Stiftelse                               NA      Foundation

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: naeringskode1 field uses Standard Industrial Classification (SN2007, based NACE Rev. 2). brreg API switched SN2025 (NACE Rev. 2.1) September 2025. Use brreg_harmonize_nace() map two. three NACE codes may registered per entity (nace_1, nace_2, nace_3). primary code (nace_1) determines industry classification.
code: # Translate NACE codes to English descriptions brreg_entity("923609016") |> brreg_label(code = "nace_1") #> nace_1 = "Extraction of crude petroleum" #> nace_1_code = "06.100"  # Or fetch the full dictionary nace <- get_brreg_dic("nace") nace[nace$code == "06.100", ]

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: antallAnsatte field reports registered employees. Important caveats: Source: NAV’s Aa-register (employer/employee register), updated ~10th month. Since September 2023, entities 0–4 employees show NA (previously showed exact count). means NA ambiguous: entity may 0–4 employees field may genuinely missing. harRegistrertAntallAnsatte (logical) distinguishes “count registered” “count registered.” ENK (sole proprietorships) typically show NA regardless actual employment.

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: entity may two addresses: forretningsadresse (business address): Legal registered address. Norwegian entities must Norwegian business address. postadresse (postal address): Mailing address. May PO box abroad. Sub-entities use beliggenhetsadresse (location address) instead forretningsadresse. address contains: adresse (street lines), postnummer (postal code), poststed (city), kommune (municipality name), kommunenummer (4-digit municipality code), land (country), landkode (ISO country code).

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: Norway reduced municipalities 428 356 January 1, 2020 (changes 2024 reverting mergers). Historical data contains old codes longer exist. Use brreg_harmonize_kommune() remap:
code: panel <- tibble::tibble(   municipality_code = c("0301", "1201", "0602"),  # Oslo, Bergen (old), Drammen (old)   year = 2019 ) brreg_harmonize_kommune(panel, target_date = "2024-01-01")

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: Key date fields meaning: registration_date reliable founding_date determining entity entered register — entities founding dates predating electronic register.

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: bulk download endpoints return different column sets: Neither format superset . package’s algorithmic unnesting produces flat tibbles formats. Choose JSON need share capital data (kapital.*) articles association text; choose CSV need dissolution details.

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: change data capture endpoints track modifications register: Enheter/Underenheter: /oppdateringer/{type} returns change events type (Ny/Endring/Sletting) optional field-level RFC 6902 patches (since September 2025). Roller: /oppdateringer/roller returns CloudEvents indicating entities role changes (field detail — fetch entity’s roles separately). Field-level patches provide new values, old values. /comparisons, use brreg_events() diff two snapshots.

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: brreg bulk files regenerated nightly. Last-Modified HTTP header download response indicates file last regenerated (typically 02:00 06:00 CET). CDC stream near-real-time business hours.

title: Norwegian business entity data
text: data Enhetsregisteret freely available Norwegian Licence Open Government Data (NLOD 2.0). API key required. formal rate limit, package applies 5 requests/second throttle courtesy.

title: Corporate governance research
text: Build bipartite graph entities persons linked board roles: tbl_graph object compatible tidygraph/ggraph ecosystem centrality analysis visualization:
code: # Select a set of entities (e.g., top energy companies) org_nrs <- c("923609016", "984851006", "985224323", "990888213")  # Build the network (requires tidygraph) net <- brreg_board_network(org_nrs) net #> # A tbl_graph: 85 nodes and 92 edges #> # An undirected multigraph with 4 components #> # Node Data: 85 × 2 (active) #>   name      node_type #>   <chr>     <chr> #> 1 923609016 entity #> 2 984851006 entity library(tidygraph) library(ggraph)  net |>   activate(nodes) |>   mutate(degree = centrality_degree()) |>   ggraph(layout = "fr") +   geom_edge_link(alpha = 0.3) +   geom_node_point(aes(size = degree, colour = node_type)) +   theme_graph()

text: large networks, pre-fetch roles pass directly:
code: all_roles <- brreg_download(type = "roller") net <- brreg_board_network(roles_data = all_roles)

title: Corporate governance research
text: Prepare time--event data compatible survival::Surv(): function applies following conventions: Entry: founding_date (stiftelsesdato) default. Override entry_var = "registration_date" registration-based entry. Exit: First non-NA among bankruptcy_date > liquidation_date > forced_dissolution_date > deletion_date (hierarchy applied order). Censoring: Firms alive censoring_date (default: today) receive event = 0. Firms exit date receive event = 1. Duration: duration_years = (exit censoring date - entry date) / 365.25. Use survival package:
code: # Download or search for a population of firms firms <- brreg_search(legal_form = "AS", municipality_code = "0301",                        max_results = 5000)  # Add survival variables surv <- brreg_survival_data(firms) surv[, c("org_nr", "entry_date", "exit_date", "duration_years", "event")] #> # A tibble: 5,000 × 5 #>   org_nr    entry_date exit_date  duration_years event #>   <chr>     <date>     <date>              <dbl> <int> #> 1 810034882 2010-01-15 NA                  15.2      0 #> 2 912345678 2015-06-01 2023-11-30           8.5      1 library(survival) km <- survfit(Surv(duration_years, event) ~ legal_form, data = surv) plot(km)

title: Building firm panels and time series
text: brreg_panel() constructs firm × period panels. target period (year-end, quarter-end, month-end), selects recent prior snapshot — LOCF (last observation carried forward). period column labels target date, snapshot_date records actual snapshot used. target date falls two snapshots, earlier snapshot carries forward.
code: # Annual panel with selected variables panel <- brreg_panel(   frequency = "year",   cols = c("employees", "nace_1", "legal_form", "municipality_code") ) panel #> # A tibble: 2,400,000 × 8 #>    org_nr    period     snapshot_date employees nace_1 legal_form is_entry is_exit #>    <chr>     <chr>      <date>            <int> <chr>  <chr>      <lgl>    <lgl> #>  1 810034882 2024-12-31 2024-07-01           10 47.110 AS         TRUE     FALSE

title: Building firm panels and time series
code: # Monthly panel brreg_panel("month", cols = c("employees"))  # Specific dates brreg_panel("custom",   dates = as.Date(c("2024-03-31", "2024-09-30")),   cols = c("employees", "nace_1") )

title: Building firm panels and time series
text: brreg_panel() adds is_entry is_exit columns: is_entry = TRUE: first period entity appears panel is_exit = TRUE: last period entity appears (bankrupt = TRUE) formal survival analysis, use brreg_survival_data() computes duration censoring indicators.

title: Building firm panels and time series
text: brreg_events() compares two specific snapshots returns entries, exits, field-level changes old new values: primary tool tracking specific field changes, gives old new values — unlike CDC stream provides new values.
code: events <- brreg_events("2024-01-01", "2025-01-01") events |> dplyr::count(event_type) #>   event_type     n #> 1 change     52314 #> 2 entry       8721 #> 3 exit        3102  # Which companies changed legal form? events |>   dplyr::filter(field == "legal_form") |>   dplyr::select(org_nr, value_from, value_to) #> # A tibble: 42 × 3 #>   org_nr    value_from value_to #>   <chr>     <chr>      <chr> #> 1 987654321 AS         ASA

title: Building firm panels and time series
text: one base snapshot want reconstruct register later date without downloading , replay CDC updates:
code: # Start with today's download base <- brreg_download(type_output = "tibble")  # Fetch changes since the snapshot updates <- brreg_updates(   since = Sys.Date(),   size = 10000,   include_changes = TRUE )  # Reconstruct state 30 days from now future_state <- brreg_replay(base, updates, target_date = Sys.Date() + 30)  attr(future_state, "replay_info") #> $n_insert: 312 #> $n_update: 1847 #> $n_delete: 45

title: Building firm panels and time series
text: Field-level changes (include_changes = TRUE) available September 2025. date, change type (Ny/Endring/Sletting) recorded. CDC patches contain new values, old values. Roller CDC (type = "roller") reports entities changed, actual role data.

title: Building firm panels and time series
text: manifest records Last-Modified header download — data vintage date (brreg regenerated file): bridge without gaps, fetch CDC updates starting 1 day Last-Modified date. creates deliberate overlap; deduplicate org_nr keeping latest event.
code: manifest <- brreg_manifest() manifest[, c("type", "snapshot_date", "last_modified")]

title: Building firm panels and time series

title: Tidy Access to Norways Business Registry (Enhetsregisteret')
text: Codes default, labels demand. Functions return Norwegian codes. brreg_label() translates English via bundled reference data live SSB Klass API lookups. Algorithmic unnesting. JSON nested objects list columns flattened atomic types. Character vectors collapsed, data frames serialized, HAL links dropped. CSV JSON paths share rename_and_coerce() — hardcoded format parity. Zero-drop policy. Unknown API fields pass auto-generated snake_case names. Schema evolution handled arrow::open_dataset(unify_schemas = TRUE). Provenance tracking. Raw download files preserved alongside processed Parquet. JSON manifest records HTTP headers (Last-Modified data vintage, ETag change detection), file hashes, record counts. Two panel construction paths. Multi-snapshot diff (brreg_panel()) historical analysis. Single snapshot + CDC replay (brreg_replay()) forward reconstruction.

title: Download the full Norwegian business register — brreg_download
text: type One "enheter" (main entities, default), "underenheter" (sub-entities / establishments), "roller" (roles entities). Roller data available JSON via /roller/totalbestand (~131 MB). format Download format: "csv" (default enheter/underenheter, semicolon-delimited) "json" (JSON array). Roller bulk download always JSON regardless parameter. refresh FALSE (default): use cached file available. TRUE: force re-download. "auto": check ETag re-download server newer version. cache Logical. TRUE (default), cache downloaded file persistently. type_output One "tibble" (default), "arrow" (requires arrow package), "path" (returns file path without parsing).

title: Download the full Norwegian business register — brreg_download
text: Depends type_output: "tibble": tibble ~1 million rows. Column names mapped via field_dict. "arrow": Arrow Table (lazy, loaded memory). "path": Character file path cached CSV.

title: Download the full Norwegian business register — brreg_download
text: brreg API two data access paths fundamentally different characteristics, following cansim (Statistics Canada) pattern separate functions separate access patterns rather eurostat pattern auto-routing within one function: brreg_search(): JSON API server-side filtering. Fast small result sets, capped 10,000. Interactive exploration. brreg_download(): Bulk CSV. Always returns full register. Appropriate panel construction, spatial joins, analysis requiring 10,000 entities. Results paths use column names via field_dict.

title: Download the full Norwegian business register — brreg_download
text: Downloaded files cached tools::R_user_dir("tidybrreg", "cache") gzipped CSV. Use refresh = TRUE force re-download, refresh = "auto" re-download cached file older latest nightly bulk export (checked via ETag headers, following cansim refresh = "auto" pattern).

Must contain org_nr, change_type, timestamp, changes (list-column patch tibbles). target_date Date. Reconstruct state date. updates timestamp <= target_date applied. cols Character vector columns track. NULL, columns present base used.

what: tidybrreg: Tidy Access to Norway's Business Registry ('Enhetsregisteret') — tidybrreg-package
title: tidybrreg: Tidy Access to Norway's Business Registry ('Enhetsregisteret') — tidybrreg-package
text: tidy interface download, search, analyze data Norwegian Central Coordinating Register Legal Entities (Enhetsregisteret), maintained Brønnøysund Register Centre. Provides access registration data approximately 1 million legal entities including companies, partnerships, sole proprietorships, associations, government bodies. Returns tibbles English column names mapped via data-driven dictionary; unknown API fields pass auto-generated names. Coded values (legal forms, industry codes, roles) can translated English demand using bundled reference data live lookups SSB Klass API. Data freely available Norwegian Licence Open Government Data (NLOD 2.0).

title: tidybrreg 0.3.1
text: brreg_network() — build entity ego-network graphs tbl_graph objects. Depth 0 (seed ), depth 1 (sub-units, children, roles, legal roles via API), depth 2 (board interlocks via local bulk data). Extensible collector pattern future relationship types. brreg_underenheter() — convenience wrapper get sub-units (BEDR/AAFY) belonging parent entity. brreg_children() — get child enheter organisational hierarchy (e.g. Stortinget → Riksrevisjonen). brreg_status() — check local bulk data availability three registry types.

title: tidybrreg 0.3.1
text: brreg_entity() now defaults registry = "auto", trying enheter first falling back underenheter 404. Output gains registry column. Explicit registry = "enheter" registry = "underenheter" skips fallback. Bulk data resolution uses Arrow lazy-load three types (roller-). Session cache .brregEnv avoids re-reading parquet files across repeated calls. Per-type lazy pipeline depth-2 expansion early-exits new entities discovered.

title: tidybrreg 0.3.1
text: Docker CI matrix simplified R 4.4.1 (R 4.3.3 image never built; multi-version coverage via standard R-CMD-check).

title: tidybrreg 0.3.0
text: pkgdown site 10 reference groups deployed GitHub Pages. 5 vignettes: Getting started, Norwegian business data, Building firm panels, Corporate governance research, Package architecture. ARCHITECTURE.md (390 lines) documenting full data flow. CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, GitHub issue templates. Hex sticker logo man/figures/logo.svg. Lifecycle experimental badge README. r-universe registration binary installs. Install instructions updated: pak (recommended), r-universe, remotes.

title: tidybrreg 0.3.0
text: New test files brreg_manifest(), brreg_replay(), brreg_series(), as_brreg_tsibble().

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_snapshot() downloads saves dated bulk register extracts Hive-partitioned Parquet files. Supports type = "enheter", "underenheter", "roller" (via /roller/totalbestand). Raw .gz files preserved alongside processed Parquet provenance. brreg_import() adds user-supplied historical CSVs snapshot partitions, normalizing column names via field_dict. brreg_snapshots() lists available snapshots dates, sizes, paths. brreg_open() opens partitioned dataset lazy Arrow Dataset (requires arrow package). brreg_data_dir() returns snapshot store path. Override options(brreg.data_dir = "/custom/path"). brreg_cleanup() prunes old partitions count age.

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_manifest() reads JSON provenance catalog recording every download: endpoint URL, download timestamp, Last-Modified header (data vintage date), ETag, file hash, record count, file paths. Used bridge snapshots CDC updates without gaps.

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_panel() constructs firm x period panels annual, quarterly, monthly, custom cadence accumulated snapshots. Uses LOCF (last observation carried forward) date resolution. brreg_replay() reconstructs register state arbitrary dates base snapshot + CDC update stream. Applies Ny/Endring/Sletting events chronologically via dplyr::rows_upsert() pattern. brreg_events() diffs two snapshots: entries, exits, field changes old new values. brreg_series() computes aggregate time series combination variables (.vars), summary functions (.fns), grouping columns (). Output columns named {variable}_{function}. as_brreg_tsibble() converts panel series output tsibble regular = FALSE tidyverts ecosystem.

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_entity() brreg_search() gain registry = "underenheter" parameter sub-entity (establishment) lookups search. brreg_roles_legal() performs reverse role lookup: roles entity X hold entities (parent, shareholder, partner).

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_download() supports type = "roller" via /roller/totalbestand (131 MB gzipped JSON). brreg_download(format = "json") downloads full JSON bulk enheter/underenheter via /enheter/lastned. Algorithmic JSON unnesting flattens list columns atomic types (character vectors collapsed, data frames serialized, HAL links dropped). Shared rename_and_coerce() pipeline CSV JSON paths.

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_harmonize_kommune() remaps municipality codes across Norway’s 2020 municipal reform 2024 county reversals using SSB KLASS. brreg_harmonize_nace() remaps NACE SN2007 SN2025 via SSB KLASS.

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_board_network() builds director interlock networks tbl_graph objects (requires tidygraph). brreg_survival_data() prepares firm survival data time--event right-censoring indicators compatible survival::Surv().

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: brreg_updates(type = "roller") fetches role change events CloudEvents format (different schema enheter/underenheter).

title: tidybrreg 0.2.0
text: Added jsonlite Imports (roller bulk JSON parsing). Added nanoparquet (>= 0.3.0), tsibble Suggests. Removed igraph, plm, fixest, collapse, sf, ggraph Suggests.

title: tidybrreg 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Entity lookup (brreg_entity()), filtered search (brreg_search()), board/officer roles (brreg_roles(), brreg_board_summary()). Full register bulk download (brreg_download()). Incremental CDC updates (brreg_updates()). Code--label translation (brreg_label(), get_brreg_dic()). Organization number validation (brreg_validate()). Reference datasets: field_dict, legal_forms, role_types, role_groups.
